You already for this bomb of I'm a bad bad bad bad already. Are you already for this bomb? I am about to drop I can't even English today and it is that people do not need to be educated They need to be guided Seldom does a client actually require any academic Information on nutrition or exercise. They know it's good for them. They know they should care about it and that's good enough People know what they already need to know People know they need protein vitamins minerals water exercise people know they need to sleep cut back on drinking not smoke Pounding information about this stuff into people is redundant and never solves a problem However, if they ask about it, you must always answer to the best of your ability What people don't know about academic stuff, they probably don't need to know People don't need to know biochemistry People don't need to know kinesiology and neither do you neither do you many times Many times People also know what they already need to do. That's the kicker, right? We're all looking about the practical side of things here and as a practitioner You were here to shrink the gap That the client has between thinking about doing something and actually doing it That gap between thinking and action is inversely correlated to the levels of motivation In other words, the more motivated a person is the more likely they're going to act in a way to get what they want Does that make sense? Does that make sense? So to reiterate the practitioner's job is to help a client generate a plan and help them systemize the implementation of that plan The practitioner's objective is to shrink the gap between what the client wants and acting on it And to simplify the lives of both parties involved Your job Both of your jobs is to close the gap between intention and intervention That is it
